review.summary: Two largely parallel route handlers with significant code duplication (sanitization, validation logic, timeout management). The core logic is sound with good defensive patterns, but there are bugs in the regex matching for bracketed terms, a DRY violation that will cause drift, and the first route has an inconsistent retry path that can silently double API cost without the `let` declaration it needs.

issues.description: The regex uses `\b` (word boundary) for all terms, but bracketed terms like `[work style` start with `[` which is not a word character. `\b` before `\[` will never match at a word boundary if preceded by a letter or digit — but more critically, if the bracketed term appears at the start of the matched region (after `[^.!?]*`), the `\b` assertion between a non-word char in the character class match and the `[` will also behave unexpectedly. The bracketed placeholders will likely never be matched by this regex, defeating their purpose as a safety net.
issues.fix: Use a conditional boundary: if the term starts with `[`, don't wrap it with `\b`. Or simply omit `\b` for bracket-prefixed terms.

issues.description: The sentence-matching regex `[^.!?]*\bTERM\b[^.!?]*[.!?]` requires a trailing sentence-ending punctuation. If the sensitive term appears in the last sentence of the document and the document doesn't end with `.`, `!`, or `?`, the sentence won't be matched and the term will slip through the filter. Markdown lens documents may end with a list item, heading, or unpunctuated paragraph.
issues.fix: Modify the regex to also match to end-of-string: `[^.!?]*\bTERM\b[^.!?]*(?:[.!?]|$)` — or run a secondary check for any remaining sensitive terms after sentence removal.

issues.location: /api/synthesize route.js — Phase 1 retry block, line with `lensDoc = await callAnthropic(...)`
issues.description: After the initial synthesis, if `sectionHeadingCount < 4`, the code retries. But `lensDoc` is declared with `const` implicitly (via `let lensDoc` in the try block). Actually looking again — `let lensDoc` is declared inside the try block scope, and the retry assigns to the same variable, which works. However, the retry uses identical inputs (same system prompt, same user content, same max_tokens) with no variation. This is unlikely to produce a different result and just burns API cost/time. The rc-synthesize route does NOT retry at all — it just returns an error. This inconsistency means one route wastes budget on a likely-futile retry while the other fails fast.
issues.fix: Either add a variation to the retry (e.g., a slightly different system prompt addendum saying 'ensure all sections are present') or remove the retry to match rc-synthesize's fail-fast behavior. At minimum, document why retrying with identical inputs would work.

issues.location: Both files — entire sanitizeLensOutput, buildSentencePattern, SENSITIVE_TERMS, validation logic, timeout management
issues.description: ~150 lines of identical logic are duplicated between the two route files (SENSITIVE_TERMS array, buildSentencePattern, sanitizeLensOutput, validation gate logic, timeout budget helpers). This is a significant DRY violation that will inevitably cause drift — if a new sensitive term is added to one file but not the other, or if a regex bug is fixed in one but not both. The project context notes system prompts are already hardcoded in JSX files and must be manually synced; this is the same pattern.
issues.fix: Extract shared utilities into a common module (e.g., `_lib/sanitize.js`, `_lib/anthropic-client.js`, `_lib/validation-gate.js`) and import in both routes.

issues.description: The body has already been parsed from JSON via `request.json()`. Re-stringifying it to check size is wasteful and the re-stringified version may differ from the original payload size (key ordering, whitespace). For a true size check, use `request.headers.get('content-length')` or check before parsing.
issues.fix: Use the Content-Length header for a rough check, or accept the current approach as 'good enough' but note it's approximate.

issues.description: The SSE streaming parser splits on `\n` and processes lines, but SSE events can be split across TCP chunks. A `data: {json}` line could be split across two `reader.read()` calls, causing a JSON parse failure on a partial line. The silent `catch(e)` swallows this, and the next chunk won't have the beginning of the line. This could cause missing text in the lens document.
issues.fix: Buffer incomplete lines across chunks. Keep a `buffer` variable and only process complete lines (those followed by a newline).

issues.description: The /api/synthesize route checks payload size (200KB limit) but the rc-synthesize route has no such check. A malicious or buggy client could send an arbitrarily large payload.
issues.fix: Add the same payload size validation as the synthesize route.

issues.description: The term 'ADD' will match the common English word 'add' (case-insensitive regex with 'gi' flag). Sentences like 'These experiences add depth to...' will be incorrectly removed. Similarly, 'OCD' is less problematic but 'Dominance' could appear in legitimate career contexts (e.g., 'market dominance').
issues.fix: Consider requiring uppercase-only matching for abbreviations (remove the 'i' flag and handle case sensitivity per-term), or use more specific patterns like 'ADD ' followed by context, or switch to phrase matching (e.g., 'diagnosed with ADD', 'has ADD').

issues.description: The 5-second floor on getRemainingTimeout means even when the deadline is exceeded, the code will still attempt a 5-second API call. This is intentional as a 'best effort' but could cause Vercel to hard-kill the function. Consider returning 0 or throwing when truly out of time.
issues.fix: Consider adding a hard bail-out when remaining time is truly exhausted (e.g., < 2000ms), rather than always allowing a 5s API call.

issues.description: The API key is passed via closure. Consider extracting the Anthropic client helper to a shared module that accepts config, improving testability and reducing duplication.
issues.fix: Create a shared `_lib/anthropic.js` module with a `callAnthropic(opts)` function.

missed_edge_cases: If `request.json()` throws (malformed JSON body), the error is caught by the outer try/catch and returns a generic 500 — consider a specific 400 response for parse errors

If the Anthropic API returns valid JSON but with `content: []` (empty array), `data.content?.[0]?.text` returns undefined, which is handled — but `data.content` being null/absent is also possible and handled correctly
Sensitive terms inside markdown code blocks (e.g., backtick-wrapped text) will still be matched and sentences removed, potentially breaking code block formatting
The sanitization regex could remove sentences from YAML frontmatter if sensitive terms appear there, potentially corrupting the lens document structure
If both validation AND re-synthesis timeout, the original (potentially hallucinated) lens is returned with only the post-processing filter as protection
positive_observations: Excellent timeout budget management with progressive degradation — validation and re-synthesis are gracefully skipped under time pressure rather than causing failures
Belt-and-suspenders approach to sensitive content: prompt instructions → validation gate → hard post-processing filter is a robust defense-in-depth strategy
Good error handling throughout — API errors, parsing failures, and re-synthesis failures all have sensible fallback behavior
System prompts kept server-side only (imported from _prompts modules), consistent with security requirements
Thoughtful truncation of rawDocumentText to keep synthesis within fast-path token budgets, with clear documentation of the reasoning
known_bug_check: System prompts hardcoded in JSX files / manual sync required: These routes correctly import prompts from dedicated `_prompts/` modules rather than hardcoding them. However, the SENSITIVE_TERMS list and sanitization logic IS hardcoded and duplicated across both route files — this is the same anti-pattern and will require manual sync.
Dark theme aesthetic patterns: No UI/styling code present in these routes — not affected.
Guardrails.yaml extraction: The sensitive terms list and validation gate logic in these files are prime candidates for extraction into a guardrails config. Currently hardcoded.
Client-side Claude API fetch: These routes correctly proxy through server-side — the API key is only accessed via process.env, never exposed to client. This is the correct pattern.
